ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station in meters */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the weather station */,
  "ground_level" REAL /* Ground level of the weather station */,
  "ground_type" TEXT /* Type of ground surface (e.g., concrete, grass, asphalt) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather forecast */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station for which the forecast is made */,
  "mdt" TEXT /* Date of the weather forecast */,
  "temp_f" REAL /* Predicted temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Predicted humidity */,
  "wind_speed_mph" REAL /* Predicted wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "precipitation_in" REAL /* Predicted precipitation in inches */,
  "cloud_cover" INTEGER /* Predicted cloud cover */,
  "events" TEXT /* Predicted weather events */,
  "wind_dir_degrees" INTEGER /* Predicted wind direction in degrees */,
  "forecast_quality_score" REAL /* Quality score of the forecast */,
  "forecast_quality_message" TEXT /* Quality message of the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather event */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the weather event (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of weather event (e.g., thunderstorm, rain, tornado) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, researcher, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., web, mobile, API) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of weather model (e.g., global, regional) */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the weather model (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_models" (
  "forecast_model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecasting model */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather model used for forecasting */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station for which the forecast is made */,
  "mdt" TEXT /* Date of the forecasting model */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the generated forecast */,
  "forecast_run" TEXT /* Run of the forecast model (e.g., 00Z, 12Z) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_models_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES weather_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_models_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES weather_forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_models_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_warnings" (
  "warning_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather warning */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station issuing the warning */,
  "mdt" TEXT /* Date of the weather warning */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather event triggering the warning */,
  "warning_message" TEXT /* Warning message */,
  "warning_level" TEXT /* Level of the warning (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warning_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_warnings_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_warnings_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather alert */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the alert */,
  "warning_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather warning triggering the alert */,
  "send_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was sent */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., SMS, email, mobile app) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_alerts_warning_id FOREIGN KEY ("warning_id") REFERENCES weather_warnings ("warning_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality metric */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being evaluated */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Quality score */,
  "quality_message" TEXT /* Quality message */,
  "quality_category" TEXT /* Category of quality (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_evaluation" (
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each evaluation metric */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather forecast being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_score" REAL /* Evaluation score */,
  "evaluation_message" TEXT /* Evaluation message */,
  "evaluation_category" TEXT /* Category of evaluation (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_evaluation_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES weather_forecasts ("forecast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_station_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date of the status */,
  "status_message" TEXT /* Status message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_station_status_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)
